info.name: Safe HTML Viewer
info.summary: Preview HTML files safely with CSP sandbox and best-effort secret redaction
info.description:
Adds a "Safe HTML preview" file action to .html / text/html files. The file opens in a new tab served under a strict Content-Security-Policy: sandbox allow-scripts allow-popups — deliberately without allow-same-origin — so embedded JavaScript can run (charts, animations, interactive reports) while staying fully isolated from your Nextcloud session, cookies, DOM and same-origin APIs.

Features

  • One-click sandboxed preview of HTML files right from the Files app
  • Respects Nextcloud permissions: only files visible to the logged-in user can be previewed
  • Best-effort display-time redaction of secrets: email addresses, phone-like strings, IPv4 addresses, private/localhost URLs, and credential-like strings (password=, token=, api_key=, long opaque tokens)
  • The original file is never modified — redaction only affects the served preview
  • No external services, no telemetry; everything runs inside your Nextcloud

Limitations: redaction is best-effort pattern matching, intended to reduce accidental secret leakage when sharing HTML documents. It is not a DLP solution and comes with no leak-prevention guarantee — see SECURITY.md in the repository.
info.author: Hiroshi Ishizaka
